  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The paper makes a specific contribution claim (ClarifySQL + clarification policy trained on AmbigSQL v3); evaluating whether that thesis is coherent and the gap statement is accurate is central to any general 'what do you think' read.",
    "R1.2": "The two-stage framing (detect then elicit/default) must hold together; the logical chain from ambiguity taxonomy → supervised training → evaluation metric → reported gains needs to be internally consistent.",
    "R1.3": "Three quantitative claims are made (73.4% accuracy, 6.1-point delta over baseline, 12% reduction in confidently-wrong SQL); each must be traceable to described experimental conditions and the stated benchmark protocol.",
    "R1.4": "The novelty claim rests on the clarification head + schema-grounded detection being a genuine delta over prior text-to-SQL and clarification work; this requires checking against the cited baselines (BIRD, Spider) and the broader NL-to-SQL literature.",
    "R1.7": "Load-bearing: the entire empirical foundation depends on 'AmbigSQL v3 (Chen et al., 2021)' being a real, verifiable resource with the described properties (4,200 added queries, five-way taxonomy, clarification-question annotations, ambiguity-weighted execution accuracy metric). If this benchmark or release does not exist as described, all reported results are unverifiable and the contribution claim collapses."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Are you asking primarily for an assessment of the paper's scholarly and empirical soundness (claims, cited benchmark existence, evidence support) rather than feedback on its writing quality and prose?"
}

rationale.R1.1: The paper makes a specific contribution claim (ClarifySQL + clarification policy trained on AmbigSQL v3); evaluating whether that thesis is coherent and the gap statement is accurate is central to any general 'what do you think' read.
rationale.R1.2: The two-stage framing (detect then elicit/default) must hold together; the logical chain from ambiguity taxonomy → supervised training → evaluation metric → reported gains needs to be internally consistent.
rationale.R1.3: Three quantitative claims are made (73.4% accuracy, 6.1-point delta over baseline, 12% reduction in confidently-wrong SQL); each must be traceable to described experimental conditions and the stated benchmark protocol.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty claim rests on the clarification head + schema-grounded detection being a genuine delta over prior text-to-SQL and clarification work; this requires checking against the cited baselines (BIRD, Spider) and the broader NL-to-SQL literature.
rationale.R1.7: Load-bearing: the entire empirical foundation depends on 'AmbigSQL v3 (Chen et al., 2021)' being a real, verifiable resource with the described properties (4,200 added queries, five-way taxonomy, clarification-question annotations, ambiguity-weighted execution accuracy metric). If this benchmark or release does not exist as described, all reported results are unverifiable and the contribution claim collapses.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking primarily for an assessment of the paper's scholarly and empirical soundness (claims, cited benchmark existence, evidence support) rather than feedback on its writing quality and prose?
